arguments.task: Research Xero using official and first-party pages where possible. Starting at https://developer.xero.com, find and extract evidence for: 1) one-line product/API description, 2) authentication methods supported for Xero APIs and whether OAuth 2.0 is required, 3) public API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL) and breadth of API products/endpoints, 4) access/pricing or partner/app registration requirements for developers, including whether self-serve is available, 5) rate limit information, 6) any official GitHub or MCP/server pages indicating an existing MCP integration. Prefer official developer docs, auth docs, pricing/access pages, FAQ/support docs, changelogs, and official GitHub/org pages. Return concise findings with exact source URLs and short supporting quotes.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Xero developer sources and related first-party pages.

arguments.task: On official Xero pages and official GitHub pages only, collect exact supporting evidence with page URLs for these points: 1) Xero app creation / developer signup flow and whether developers can self-serve create an app, 2) OAuth methods listed on the OAuth 2.0 overview page including authorization code, PKCE, and custom connections, plus whether custom connections require a paid Xero plan, 3) exact rate limit numbers from the limits page, 4) high-level API product breadth from the API overview page, 5) evidence that Xero has an official MCP server on GitHub. Return concise bullet findings with exact URLs and short quotes.

input.thought: Run a focused follow-up extraction to capture exact support for access tier, auth methods, limits, breadth, and MCP.

raw_result.description: Cloud accounting and business platform with APIs for accounting, payroll, assets, files, projects, and related business data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Xero docs describe OAuth 2.0 flows including authorization code, PKCE for public apps, and Custom Connections for single-organisation integrations; no verified evidence gathered for other API auth methods.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developers can access public developer documentation and app registration flows through the Xero developer platform; Custom Connections appear to require a paid Xero plan, but standard OAuth app development appears self-serve from the developer portal.

api_surface.breadth: Public API documentation shows multiple product areas and endpoints, including Accounting plus other business APIs such as payroll, assets, files, and projects; no evidence gathered of a public GraphQL surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP server appears to exist via Xero's GitHub presence and the Xero developer site AI Toolkit references.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits page indicates multiple limits including concurrent request limits, per-minute limits, daily limits, and app-wide limits; browser evidence referenced Starter/Core/Plus tiers and uncertified-app connection limits, but exact numeric values should be rechecked manually from the limits page before operationalizing.
raw_result.notable_signals: Xero has a dedicated developer portal, OAuth 2.0 docs, broad business/accounting API coverage, and an official MCP server repository under XeroAPI, all strong signals for agent/toolkit readiness.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Research session established strong directional evidence, but exact wording for self-serve app registration, exact numeric rate limits, and whether any non-OAuth auth method exists were not fully captured verbatim in the gathered output and should be manually verified from the cited official pages.
